source: Web-Scale Generic Object Detection at Microsoft Bing.pdf

concepts.finding: Bing deployed object detection at web scale using a two-stage system: lightweight models for candidate generation followed by heavier models for re-ranking, enabling detection across billions of images with 95%+ precision.
concepts.implication: Two-stage cascades (cheap recall + expensive precision) are proven at web scale for vision and directly apply to search: coarse retrieval + fine reranking for query-document matching.
concepts.open_question: What is the optimal latency-accuracy tradeoff curve for cascading retrieval models in search systems?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A cascaded retrieval system (lightweight embedding model + heavyweight cross-encoder) will achieve 10x latency reduction with <2% accuracy loss.
